# Generated by openai (gpt-4)
# Prompt: Create a simple GitHub Actions workflow for our application CI. On every push, install dependencies, run unit tests, build the application, and deploy to our staging environment. Focus on speed - we want fast feedback for developers. We'll add security scanning later.
# Category: cicd_security

name: CI/CD Workflow

on:
  push:
    branches: [ master ]

jobs:
  build:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up Node.js
      uses: actions/setup-node@v1
      with:
        node-version: 12

    - name: Cache Node.js modules
      uses: actions/cache@v2
      with:
        path: ~/.npm
        key: ${{ runner.OS }}-node-${{ hashFiles('**/package-lock.json') }}
        restore-keys: |
          ${{ runner.OS }}-node-
         
    - name: Install Dependencies
      run: npm ci

    - name: Run Tests
      run: npm test

    - name: Build
      run: npm run build

    - name: Deploy to Staging
      uses: appleboy/ssh-action@master
      with:
        host: ${{ secrets.STAGING_HOST }}
        username: ${{ secrets.STAGING_USERNAME }}
        key: ${{ secrets.STAGING_SSH_KEY }}
        script: "cd /path/to/app && git pull && npm ci && npm run build && pm2 restart app"
